name: Around the Regional Protected Landscape Park of the Serra do Porto — circular route
name_original: À volta da Paisagem Protegida Regional Parque das Serras do Porto — percurso circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valongo traverse a landscape characterized by diverse terrain, including challenging mountain ranges and serene river valleys. The region features the mountainous areas of Serra de Santa Justa, Serra de Pias, and Alto Saldanha, offering routes with significant elevation changes. Alongside the Ferreira River, more gentle paths provide a contrast to the rugged uplands. Valongo also encompasses the Valongo Palaeozoic Park, an area of geological significance with historical mining sites. Best touring cycling routes around Valongo The most popular touring cycling route is Around the Regional Protected Landscape Park of the Serra do Porto — circular route, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ride, often on paved surfaces, around the protected landscape park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Senhora do Salto Waterfall – Aqueduto dos Arcos loop from Valongo, a difficult 26.1 miles (42.0 km) path. This route features the historic Arcos aqueduct and scenic river views. Local touring cyclists also love the Boavista Bike – Paupério Biscuit Factory loop from Valongo, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Valongo is defined by its mountain ranges, river valleys, and protected natural areas. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more technical courses. The routes in Valongo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 190 reviews. More than 2300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valongo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Valongo?
A: Valongo offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 120 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Valongo?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain in Valongo. The region features challenging mountain ranges like Serra de Santa Justa, Serra de Pias, and Alto Saldanha, offering routes with significant elevation gains. In contrast, paths alongside the Ferreira River provide more gentle, serene routes. Many trails offer a mix of dirt roads, forest areas, and paved surfaces, with some technical sections.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Valongo?
A: Yes, Valongo has several circular touring cycling routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379936520/around-the-regional-protected-landscape-park-of-the-serra-do-porto-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around the Regional Protected Landscape Park of the Serra do Porto — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 29.8 km ride, often on paved surfaces, offering a challenging experience around the protected landscape park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Valongo's routes are rich in natural and historical sights. You can encounter the geological wonders of the Valongo Palaeozoic Park, serene river valleys, and panoramic views from mountain peaks like Monte de Santa Justa. Historical landmarks such as the Santa Justa Chapel, the Arcos aqueduct, and the Roman bridge over the Ferreira River are also visible along various paths. For specific natural highlights, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2863992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora do Salto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4601831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico da Serra de Santa Justa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Valongo?
A: For families or less experienced cyclists, the trails alongside the Ferreira River offer more gentle and scenic routes. These paths are generally more level and provide a relaxed touring experience, perfect for enjoying the natural beauty at a leisurely pace.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Valongo?
A: Valongo offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes mean you can find suitable routes in different seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for exploring both the mountain and riverside trails. Even in winter, some trails may be accessible, but it's always best to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or aqueducts?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that pass by impressive water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora do Salto Waterfall – Aqueduto dos Arcos loop from Valongo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that features the historic Arcos aqueduct and scenic river views, leading to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2863992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora do Salto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valongo?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Valongo, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, which offers everything from challenging mountain climbs to tranquil riverside paths, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: Can I reach Valongo's cycling routes using public transport?
A: Yes, Valongo is accessible by urban trains from Porto, which can be convenient for cyclists bringing their bikes. This allows for easier access to the starting points of various routes within the region.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by interesting local businesses or points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes offer a glimpse into local life and industry. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32216501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boavista Bike – Paupério Biscuit Factory loop from Valongo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.3 km trail that leads through varied terrain, potentially offering views or proximity to local establishments like the biscuit factory.

Q: Where can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Valongo?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the mountainous areas of Serra de Santa Justa, Serra de Pias, and Alto Saldanha offer routes with significant elevation gains and technical sections. The Valongo Mountain Bike Center serves as a hub for exploring these areas, with trails like the 'Parapente climb' featuring over 400 meters of elevation gain. Many of the 69 difficult routes in the region will provide a strenuous adventure.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Valongo?
A: Absolutely. The top of Monte de Santa Justa, the highest point of the Serra de Santa Justa and the Valongo Massif, provides beautiful panoramic views. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32268749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meco Summit Viewpoint loop from Sobrado&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to lead to scenic overlooks, allowing you to enjoy the stunning landscapes of the region.
tips: The Parque das Serras do Porto Regional Protected Landscape, located in the Porto Metropolitan Area, is a biodiversity refuge and provides important ecosystem services to the community. The area comprises six mountains (Santa Justa, Pias, Castiçal, Santa Iria, Flores and Banjas), interspersed with the valleys of the Ferreira and Sousa rivers.
At the top of Monte de Santa Justa, the culmination of the Serra de Santa Justa and the Massif de Valongo, there is a beautiful panorama.
